verdict_reasoning: HMGB1 is a canonical nuclear/chromatin protein with no TM domain, no GPI anchor, and no leader sequence for conventional secretion. Its extracellular form is a released soluble DAMP that acts on RAGE, TLR2, TLR4, and CXCR4 on other cells — it is not itself stably displayed on the outer leaflet of the releasing cell. Anti-HMGB1 therapeutic antibodies target the circulating/soluble extracellular pool (analogous to anti-cytokine programs), not a cell-surface-anchored protein body. Pre-no checklist: (1) cell_state_induced — GeneCards notes 'found on surface of activated platelets' and one review mentions nucleus-to-cell-surface localisation in stress/cancer, but no gene-specific surface-biotinylation or flow-cytometry-on-intact-non-permeabilized-cells evidence establishes a wash-resistant outer-leaflet pool; the 'surface' association is likely reversible lipid/receptor binding that washes off. (2) tissue_restricted_surface — no narrow-lineage stable outer-leaflet display documented with its own anchor. (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — secretory-lysosome pathway results in release into extracellular space, not stable PM residence. (4) dual_localization — no documented stable minority PM pool with its own anchor. (5) stable_surface_attachment — no covalent or wash-resistant tethering to a TM partner documented. Verdict: secreted_only (released soluble DAMP).
predicted_key_uncertainty: GeneCards cites surface display on activated platelets (PubMed:11154118) and one review mentions cell-surface localisation in cancer; the wash-resistance and mechanism of this putative surface association are not established, leaving a low residual probability of contextual/cell_state_induced.
